mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.12% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market demand that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 142,654 thousand suggests near-full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to decline simultaneously to confirm demand destruction.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the correlation between joint RSAFS-HOUST declines and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining below its 3-month moving average signals supply-side weakness and capacity utilization stress that constrains profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO dips below 3-month MA are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This mechanism is regime-conditional and most powerful during early-cycle downturns.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 5.0% near full employment
regime_hint: early-stage labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of instances when UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during recession-adjacent periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This is an always-on trend signal.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 16,394.2 billion chained 2017 dollars
regime_hint: any regime with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of GDPC1 growth > 2.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 2.0% signals real rate compression that erodes equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both conditions to activate.
regime_hint: stagflation or monetary policy lag regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of high inflation and low rates does not produce significantly negative returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,100 thousand units annualized signals residential demand collapse that portends broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, particularly in construction-sensitive and cyclical equity sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST at 1,206 thousand units near trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when HOUST < 1,100 thousand, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during housing-sensitive periods.
